title: Pyrefly configuration
description: Configuration file schema for Pyrefly, a fast Python type checker.

baseline.description: Path to baseline file for comparing type errors. Errors matching the baseline are suppressed.

conda-environment.description: Name of the Conda environment to use.

disable-project-excludes-heuristics.description: Whether to filter out the required excludes or filter things in your site package path.

disable-search-path-heuristics.description: Disable Pyrefly default heuristics around constructing a modified search path.

disable-type-errors-in-ide.description: Disables type errors from showing up when running Pyrefly in an IDE.

enabled-ignores.description: What set of tools should Pyrefly respect ignore directives from?

errors.description: Configure the severity for each kind of error that Pyrefly emits.

fallback-python-interpreter-name.description: A generic command name (e.g. 'python3') to resolve via `which` into a python_interpreter path.

ignore-errors-in-generated-code.description: Whether to ignore type errors in generated code.

ignore-missing-imports.description: Instruct Pyrefly to replace the given module globs with typing.Any and ignore import errors for the module only when the module can't be found.

infer-with-first-use.description: Whether to infer type variables not determined by a call or constructor based on their first usage.

permissive-ignores.description: Should Pyrefly ignore errors based on annotations from other tools?

project-excludes.description: Files that should be excluded as sources. Takes precedence over project-includes.

project-includes.description: Files that should be counted as sources (e.g. user-space code).

python-interpreter-path.description: Path to the Python interpreter to use for type checking.

recursion-depth-limit.description: Maximum recursion depth for type evaluation. Set to 0 to disable the limit.

recursion-overflow-handler.description: How should Pyrefly handle recursion overflow during type evaluation?

replace-imports-with-any.description: Instruct Pyrefly to unconditionally replace the given module globs with typing.Any and ignore import errors for the module.

search-path.description: The list of directories where imports are imported from.

site-package-path.description: A list of directory paths describing roots from which imports should be found and imported from.

skip-interpreter-query.description: Whether to skip querying an interpreter for site-packages and stdlib paths.

skip-lsp-config-indexing.description: Whether to let Pyrefly try to index the project's files. Disabling may speed up LSP operations on large projects.

sub-config.description: Sub-configs that can override specific settings based on path matching.

tensor-shapes.description: Whether to enable tensor shape checking.

typeshed-path.description: Override the bundled typeshed with a custom path.

untyped-def-behavior.description: How should Pyrefly treat function definitions with no parameter or return type annotations?

oneOf.description: Args and settings for querying a custom source DB.

command.description:
The command to run. Pyrefly will call this in the form `<command> @<argfile>`, where `<argfile>` has the format ```text -- <arg-flag> <arg> ... ```

`<arg-flag>` is either `--file` or `--target`, depending on the type of `<arg>`, and `<arg>` is an absolute path to a file or a build system's target.

repo-root.description: The root of the repository. Repo roots here will be shared between configs.

search-path-prefix.description: Are there any sources we should use before looking at the build system (like stubs)?

ErrorDisplayConfig.description: Configure the severity for each kind of error that Pyrefly emits. Set error code to true to enable (default severity), false to disable, or use a severity string.

PythonPlatform.description: The value used with conditions based on type checking against sys.platform values.

PythonVersion.description: The Python version to use for type checking. Format: <major>[.<minor>[.<micro>]]

RecursionOverflowHandler.description: How to handle when recursion depth limit is exceeded.

oneOf.description: Return a placeholder type and emit an internal error. Safe for IDE use.
Dump debug info to stderr and panic. For debugging stack overflow issues.

SubConfig.description: Sub-configuration that overrides settings for files matching a glob pattern.

disable-type-errors-in-ide.description: Disables type errors from showing up when running Pyrefly in an IDE.

enabled-ignores.description: What set of tools should Pyrefly respect ignore directives from?

errors.description: Configure the severity for each kind of error that Pyrefly emits.

ignore-errors-in-generated-code.description: Whether to ignore type errors in generated code.

ignore-missing-imports.description: Instruct Pyrefly to replace the given module globs with typing.Any and ignore import errors for the module only when the module can't be found.

infer-with-first-use.description: Whether to infer type variables not determined by a call or constructor based on their first usage.

permissive-ignores.description: Should Pyrefly ignore errors based on annotations from other tools?

recursion-depth-limit.description: Maximum recursion depth for type evaluation. Set to 0 to disable the limit.

recursion-overflow-handler.description: How should Pyrefly handle recursion overflow during type evaluation?

replace-imports-with-any.description: Instruct Pyrefly to unconditionally replace the given module globs with typing.Any and ignore import errors for the module.

tensor-shapes.description: Whether to enable tensor shape checking.

untyped-def-behavior.description: How should Pyrefly treat function definitions with no parameter or return type annotations?

Tool.description: The name of the tool that is being suppressed. Note that the variant names and docstrings are displayed in `pyrefly check --help`.

oneOf.description: Enables `# pyrefly: ignore` and `# pyrefly: ignore-errors`
Enables `# pyre: ignore`, `# pyre-ignore`, `# pyre-fixme`, and `# pyre-ignore-all-errors`
